title: Progressive International Prepworks Jar Grips Multi-Purpose Lid Openers, Set of 3
description:
About this item
Open Stubborn Jars Of All Sizes: Three Durable Jar Grips Provide Superior Grip When Opening Stubborn Jars And Containers Of All Sizes
Three Differnt Shapes: Comes In Three Individual Shapes And Colors For Every Kind Of Jar
Innovative Design: Teal Square And Green Round Flat Grip For Large Jars And Red Cone Shaped Ribbed Grip For Smaller Bottles And Jars
Makes Opening Jars Effortless: Takes The Effort Out Of Opening Jars And Removing Lids
› See more product details
reviews_5s:
I use the two flat grippers all the time - they work great for opening jars. I've had them for more than three years now and none of them show any signs of tearing or wearing out yet.

As has been noted by some of the reviewers who gave this a lower rating, the two flat ones are thin. I don't see that as a downside in terms of getting a good grip on a jar lid - on the contrary it seems to let them get a better hold because they easily conform to any jar's contours. I'm not sure why it would help for them to be thicker, since the idea is just to create better friction between a smooth and potentially moist jar and a smooth and potentially moist hand. Any thickness of rubbery, textured material should work for that.

The green one is large enough for a standard peanut butter or pickle jar, and the blue one is large enough for any oversized jar I've managed to buy in the past 3 years. I suppose there are probably jars in the world for which it would be too small. Perhaps if I were in the habit of buying those giganti-sized jars of three-bean salad at Costco I wouldn't be able to use these to open them. If I ever do encounter a jar lid of that scale I might try one of those place-mat-sized sheets of spongy shelf liner, or maybe a scrap of that non-slip stuff that's sold to put under rugs. But if the jar were that large I suspect my hand would be too small to reach around the lid, so it may be moot that the blue thingy would be too small.

Sure, these are bound to wear out eventually. So far mine don't show any tendency to develop rips or holes. I bought these to replace an older one that did wear out even though it was slightly thicker. After several years its material became less flexible and started to develop holes. I think it's just the nature of rubber materials to eventually degrade due to exposure to air and light. My expectation is I'll need to buy a new set of these in a few more years, but at less than $1 per year that's not an unreasonable investment.

The red cone shaped thing is much thicker, and has ribs to enhance its gripping powers, but I don't get much use out of that one. I think it's intended to open twisty bottle caps, but I seem to have enough strength to handle most of those bare-handed, so rarely think to use it.These jar grips may be old school but they work well and don't take up a ton of room. I recommend this product.I’ve had this for two years and they are very effective for opening jar and bottle tops. It’s important to realise that these grippers only make it easier for you to hold on to the jar and its top without them slipping out of your hand - which is all that is usually needed. They do not open jar tops magically. You certainly can use rubber bands that are of the right width and thickness but I for one can’t be bothered to hunt round for rubber bands and fiddle with making sure they stay in place at the rim of the top I want to open. The very thin gripper might well tear but it’s a tradeoff. Sometimes you do need something very thin to fit some very small screw tops. If you have trouble with dexterity or finger and wrist strength, then you need a gadget designed for people with such problems. I’m 74 and for particularly tight lids, I use something else which is more clunky. These grips are handy for everyday use.I mainly use these in the kitchen to open jars that I can't open with my hand. I will use the blue and green ones to keep stuff from sliding around on the counter, but the red one is best for opening jars. My mom actually recommended these and I am forever grateful to her because I don't know how I use to function without these before.They really work and my hand doesn't feel like the skin is being ripped off!I'm starting to have some real problems with osteoarthritis in my hands, so removing jar lids can be a major challenge. I've tried several different openers -- various engineering marvels that are supposed to latch onto the lid and remove it easily -- but they often don't work as advertised OR getting them set up causes more problems.

These "jar grips" are a quick and easy low-tech solution. I usually use two of them -- the red "cone" one for the lid plus the blue square to grab the jar -- and in almost all cases, I can get the lid off without the pain and frustration I normally experience. I typically use them on things like jars of preserves, pickles, olives, salsa, etc. So far, no problems at all, no signs of wear and tear, still performing just fine after about 8 weeks of use, maybe 3-4 times a week.

I paid less than five dollars for the set and to me, they're well worth it.Older person. Grip is getting weaker. This makes opening a bottle or jar a snap.I really like these rubber grippers by Progressive. I am replacing the new ones with my older Tupperware rubber grippers. The only difference is that these are slightly thinner and smaller than my original Tupperware grippers. Not enough difference not to like these rubber grippers made by Progressive. I previously ordered from a different seller a pack of rubber grippers that looked a lot like these Progressive grippers, but the other seller's rubber grippers were made from another company and they were a little thicker and had a very slick surface and we couldn't grip anything no matter how hard we tried. I ended up not trying to use them anymore. Now back to these Progressive rubber grippers, we really like using them and will keep this seller and company on our re-order list. Thank you.Super la inversiones y la calidad, mas que recomendado abre lo que seaKnow I can open my jars
reviews_1s:
How does anyone think these are good?

These are only good for lids that would have come off anyway in your bare hands with a little more effort.

I tried to open a jar of honey with one of the flat openers. The rubber started wrinkling and tearing apart under my grip, so I tried the second flat one.

That opener started tearing apart even worse. The rubber would quickly turn white where it was being stretched and then would pull apart and wrinkle in on itself as I twisted. It was impossible to apply my full force because the rubber couldn’t handle it.

The third one that looks like a cupcake cup is the best and the thickest of the three but even that one deformed so as to have a permanent bulge outward in the shape of the lid. Its awkward shape makes it difficult to use when opening a standard can lid because you can’t really leverage your palm against it.

I was finally able to get the jar open when I grabbed a rubber resistance exercise band, wrapped it around the lid and gave a quick twist. It was NOT because the previous attempts loosened it.Package was sealed but should have been (3) rubber jar openers but only received (2) inside package.This is rubber. They work well enough, but I put one in the dishwasher and bits came off and stuck like cement to everything else! That was weeks ago, and I'm still finding blue stuff, so I think pieces are stuck in the dishwasher somewhere, only to find their way to my dishes again and again.

Try silicone. They work great and last much longer.

𝗡𝗢𝗧 RECOMMENDED.these are awful. I bought because of all the positive reviews. They pretty much broke right away or just don't work. I have had 2 random ones that have lasted about 15 years before they started tearing so I needed to replace. I replaced with these and they tore quickly. These are much thinner than my original ones. I'm still on the search for a thicker one closer to my original. But, pass on these.Except for the round sheet of textured rubber, these don't work and slip all over the place. Wonder if they'd be safe as cat toys ... ?I have another lid opener bigger than this one and wanted a smaller one. I've had my blue one 20+ years and it's as good as new. This red one is actually too small. Plus it slips on the lid unless you use great pressure which was the reason for wanting it--my naked hand does just as poorly at opening jar lids. Plus it is stiff and thick and therefore, clumsy. The other 2 flat ones are even more useless. All three are in my GoodWill box.Beware - not made in USA. Bought because it says made in USA but when arrived clearly says "Designed in USA, made in China". Very deceptive. Will be returning.FOR A SENIOR CITIZEN WHO NEEDS HELP OPENING JARS AND BOTTLE CAPS, TRYING TO OPEN THIS PACKAGE WAS A JOKE. FOR JAR GRIPPERS THIS PACKAGING WENT TOO FAR. A LITTLE PULL TAB ON THE BACK WAS ANOTHER JOKE. SOMEONE WILL BE OVER IS A COUPLE OF WEEKS AND WILL OPEN IT FOR ME.
I'M SURE THEY'LL WORK FINE ONCE UNPACKED. MY OTHERS WORE OUT.THIS PRODUCT SUCKED!!!! It's really thin!!!!! It did all that damage with just one thing that I was trying to open, I ended up opening what I need with a dish washing glove. Normally it's made out of a little thicker material but this one is very thin AND WILL NOT LAST LONG...WAIST OF MONEY!!!!These did t work for me.
